Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
Incident Summary I was defrauded of {$1000.00} by an individual impersonating a law-enforcement officer.

Details of the Incident XXXX XX/XX/year> at XXXX AM, I received a phone call from a man who identified himself as a XXXX. He stated that I had failed to appear for jury duty and that a warrant had been issued for my arrest. He followed up with an email containing what appeared to be official court documents and claimed I owed a XXXX  fine. 

The caller said the fine would be {$2000.00} and pressured me to pay immediately. I told him that my bank only allows me to send {$1000.00} per day. He then said he would see what he could do about dropping XXXX of the supposed counts against me for not showing up to jury duty, and instructed me to pay {$1000.00} that day. 

He further directed me to make the payment using my last name and a juror number as the reference, to make it appear legitimate. Believing the communication to be official, I sent a total of {$1000.00} as directed. 

After sending the money, I was locked out of my account. I immediately attempted to cancel the Zelle transfer and spent over an hour and a half on the phone with XXXX Bank. I also went in person to the bank, but was informed that nothing could be done because the Zelle payment had already moved past the pending phase and could not be canceled. 

After the payment was completed, the caller became evasive and ultimately terminated the call. At that point I realized I had been the victim of a scam. 

Contact Information Used by Scammer Phone numbers : XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX, XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX Email : XXXX Payment Information Payment method : Zelle Amount : {$1000.00} Recipient name : XXXX XXXX XXXX phone ( enrolled with Zelle ) : ( XXXX ) XXXX Bank : XXXX XXXX ( sending bank ) Transaction confirmation number : XXXX Payment reference : Last name & juror number provided by scammer Additional Action Taken I reported this incident to my local police department. A police report is currently being created and will be available in a few days, which I can provide as supporting evidence.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
XXXX reporting identity theft and Cash Apps failure to resolve it after more than 100 days of repeated attempts to get help. I never signed up for Cash App or requested a card, yet an account was opened in my name without my consent and a debit card was mailed to me. 

Since XX/XX/XXXX, I have : 1. Reported the fraud immediately upon receiving the card 2. Uploaded photos of the cards front and back, masking sensitive info, multiple times at their request.

3. Repeatedly called Cash App, only to be given different case numbersat least fiveand told to resubmit the same information each time.

4. Tried to get help through Cash Apps XXXX messaging portal with no success. 

5. XXXX XXXX Bank ( card issuer ), which has proven the most helpful so far. They agreed to initiate closing the card but I have yet to see if the account will be closed. 
However, XXXX Bank does not have access to the Cash App account itself since they only issue the cards. They advised me to contact Cash App directly to address the fraudulent activity Despite all this, I still have no confirmation the fraudulent account is closed, no detailed record of any account activity, no explanation of how my identity was used to open the account, and no assurance my personal data has been removed from their systems. 

When I ask Cash App representatives : 1. What the next steps are, they dont know.

2. What kind of communication or updates I can expect, they say they cant tell me.

3. What the timeline for resolution is, they refuse to provide XXXX. 

For transparency or escalation, XXXX met with silence or worsea rep hung up on me. 

The constant opening of new case numbers and repeated requests for the same information show Cash App has no clear process to follow through. Its baffling how banks and credit card companies can give immediate answers about fraud activity and next steps, yet Cash App has done nothing for over XXXX months. When I talk to them, they say " they're working on it still. '' This ongoing lack of response and transparency has been incredibly frustrating and stressful. 

I am demanding clear answers, official confirmation that my identity is protected, and permanent closure of this fraudulent account.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
In XXXX, I was the victim of wire fraud that could have been prevented had JPMorgan Chase Bank followed basic verification procedures. The transaction was related to the purchase of residential land in Florida. I selected a property that was offered by a XXXX XXXX real estate agent and initiated a wire transfer of {$18000.00} from my company to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in Florida. However, a scammer intercepted the communication by impersonating the title company through a spoofed email domain ( .corn instead of .com ). I was deceived into wiring the funds to the scammers personal account at JPMorgan Chase, which he had linked to the fraudulent instructions while using the correct beneficiarys name, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Despite the mismatch between the account holder name and the intended beneficiary name, JPMorgan Chase Bank still credited the funds to the scammers account. The bank later confirmed that the account holder name did not match the listed beneficiary which is a clear violation of standard wire transfer verification practices and UCC 4A-207. 

I reported the fraud to JPMorgan Chase Bank, and they acknowledged the discrepancy. However, they did not reverse the transaction or issue a refund. I contacted JPMorgans call center and spoke to a representative named XXXX, who confirmed that if a mismatch occurred between the beneficiarys name and account number, the funds would not be credited and would be refunded to the sender within 14 days. Unfortunately, I did not receive my money back. 

As a result, at that time I hired a dedicated attorney who followed up on the case until it was escalated to the local police. The police conducted their own investigation and sent a formal letter to JPMorgan Chase requesting clarification. JPMorgan replied with a letter stating that they needed additional time to investigate and would respond. Since then, they have not followed up neither with the police department, my attorney, nor with me. 

I am currently in the United States on a temporary visit, which has allowed me to reopen this matter with the seriousness it deserves. At the time of the fraud in XXXX, I was living XXXX  and had limited access to legal remedies in the U.S. I am now prepared to pursue all available legal and regulatory channels to seek redress and recover my funds. Based on this, I sent a formal letter to JPMorgan Chase Banks Wire Investigations Department, explaining the situation and requesting a refund. I gave them two weeks to respond or issue the refund. At the end of that period, I received a single phone call from the Executive Office, specifically from XXXX XXXX, who acknowledged the issue but refused to provide a resolution timeline. He gave me his contact number : XXXX, XXXX. XXXX. I waited an additional week without receiving any further communication. I called the number multiple times and left voicemails but never received a response. I then sent another letter and gave them an additional week again, without receiving any reply, feedback, or resolution. 

If no resolution is reached through your agency, I am prepared to pursue legal action based on JPMorgan Chases failure to apply standard wire verification safeguards. I am attaching all relevant documentation in support of my claim.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I saw a XXXX post on a friend 's account offering to sell cars and equipment. It was posed as a favor he was doing for an XXXX uncle. The original post asked for a deposit to reserve a purchase until it could be viewed at a later date. The deposit was said to be fully refundable if I decided to not purchase the cars. I reached out on XXXX XXXX, and the " friend '' said to pay the deposit via the Friends and Family feature in PayPal. I did for the amount of {$2500.00} on XX/XX/2025. There was additional money as a finance fee charged by Paypal that brought the total charge to {$2500.00} that was charged to my XXXX XXXX credit card and paid to the ultimate " seller '' via Paypal. 

The " seller '' requested more money. I became suspicious and confirmed that this was a scam. The XXXX account was hacked, and all the information was false. I can prove all these things with screenshots of the post, the friend 's wife 's post saying his account had been hacked, the promise of a refundable deposit, etc. I eventually talked to the actual friend, and he verbally confirmed on the phone that it was a scam. I tried reaching out on XXXX XXXX asking for my deposit back but was promptly blocked by the scammer. 

I submitted disputes to Paypal. There have been a few responses, documents shared since then, but little cooperation from PayPal. 

Paypal is saying that it is an " externally filed '' dispute ( to XXXX XXXX ), and that it is fully XXXX XXXX 's responsibility to remedy. They also say because the Friends and Family feature was used to " purchase '' goods, that I violated the user agreement of Paypal, and therefore have no protection. 

First, I think the user agreement that specifies these terms is difficult to find, and more importantly, vague on the point of deposits. This deposit did nothing other than act as a sign of good faith that I would look at the car ( even though it was all fiction ). There was no service or good exchanged as a result of this deposit. It was ostensibly a personal friend doing a favor for his aging family member. I don't think the position Paypal has taken regarding the Friends and Family violation is fair, and at the very least, ambiguous. 

I also argue that the " seller '' also broke the user agreement by asking for the deposit via the Friends and Family option, but from what little Paypal has shared with me, they are not being held accountable for this. 

Further, according to Paypal 's own dispute resolution system, they show that the actual " seller '' said they had refunded the money to me, which they have not. Also, when I questioned Paypal customer service about if this account had been flagged as a scammer before my transaction, they said I would have to file a subpoena to get that information. 

The scammer account is still active, and customer service was not at all inquisitive as to whether or not I had proof that it was a scam account. They only offered to accept my proof after I specifically requested that they take the information and investigate so that they can close the account and save others the trouble. 

I have no way of knowing the history of this account, or if Paypal knew this was a suspicious account before my activity. 

I've filed complaints regarding this matter with the FTC and the Internet Crime Complaint Center.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
Company : Block, Inc. ( Cash App ) Product : Mobile Wallet / Virtual Payments Issue : Fraud or scam ; unauthorized transactions ; financial loss ; failure to assist Dollar Amount Lost : Exceeds {$5000.00} What Happened At no point did I authorize these transactions. These funds were either removed from my Cash App balance without authorization, intercepted during transfer, or fraudulently redirected. I am filing this complaint because I experienced fraud through Cash App that resulted in the loss, theft, and/or interception of more than {$5000.00} in cash deposits.

Despite providing detailed evidenceincluding timestamps, transaction IDs, screenshots, and verification that the activity was not initiated by meCash App refused to restore the stolen funds or properly investigate the matter. After discovering the unauthorized activity, I immediately contacted Cash App support.

I have not been provided any meaningful investigation results, fraud documentation, or dispute resolution. Yet Cash App repeatedly responded with generic, automated messages and closed my support tickets without explanation. The fraudulent transactions were significant, sudden, and clearly inconsistent with my account history.

Steps I Took With Cash App Reported the unauthorized transactions through the app immediately.

Submitted all requested documents and screenshots. 

Verified my identity and account ownership. 

Attempted to escalate the issue through multiple support channels. 

Requested a full investigation and recovery of the stolen funds. 

Requested documentation of Cash Apps findings. 

At no time did they provide a legitimate investigation or an explanation for their decision. Cash App ignored these requests, closed the cases without resolution, or sent copy-and-paste responses stating they could not assist. 

Impact As a regulated financial service, Cash App has an obligation to investigate unauthorized transactions, provide consumers with dispute rights, and ensure that customer deposits are protected from fraud and interception. These funds were intended for essential expenses, and Cash Apps lack of support has further compounded the harm. The financial loss exceeds {$5000.00}, which has created a significant hardship.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative: On XX/XX/year>, I participated in a XXXX  interview for a remote appointment setter position with what I believed to be a legitimate company, XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( also operating as XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and previously known as XXXX XXXX XXXX  ). After further investigation, I discovered that the company had previously gone by the name XXXX XXXX XXXX and has been reported for engaging in fraudulent hiring practices. During the interview, I was asked by representatives XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX for remote access to my screen under the pretense of setting up work-related software. However, without my knowledge or consent, they used this access to open a {$6000.00} loan in my name and make unauthorized transactions through my Apple Wallet. After the interview, I was pressured into electronically signing contracts under false pretenses, with the company claiming that I needed to complete training and work for them in order to be reimbursed for the money taken from my accounts. When I tried to opt out of this situation, I was threatened with legal action if I disputed the charges. They claimed to have recorded our XXXX  interview and had my signed contract as evidence against me. After conducting further research, I found that this company has a history of misleading individuals with fraudulent pay-to-work schemes. As a result, I was defrauded of {$6000.00}, which includes {$1000.00} withdrawn from my XXXX XXXX  credit card account and {$5000.00} charged to my Apple Card, issued by Goldman Sachs Bank. These transactions were processed through the XXXX XXXX  which is a social commerce platform and when I attempted to request a refund from the merchant, my account on XXXX was suspended. In response, I immediately filed disputes on XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX with the XXXX XXXX, and Apple/Goldman Sachs Bank for the unauthorized charges. In addition, I have already filed complaints with IC3 and the FTC regarding this matter. I am now submitting this complaint to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) to seek refund and the removal of the fraudulent loan from my credit history. To support my case, I have attached emails, the signed contract, proof of transactions made to the XXXX XXXX. Please let me know if any additional information is needed. Thank you for reviewing my case and I really hope we can find a solution to this soon.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
Around XXXX of XXXX I ( XXXX XXXX ) received a phone call from XXXX asking If I opened a bank account in New York. I stated that I have not since I am located in XXXX, XXXX The " bankteller '' proceeded to tell me that there's a chance that my info got compromissed and that I need to file a police report. He then transferred me to what I thought was XXXX and I met an XXXX there named XXXX XXXX XXXX # XXXX. He then proceeded to make a police report for me, during the report he stated that I was a suspect for a Money Laundering investigation involving {$280000.00}. I stated that I was not aware of this and that this is my first time of hearing it. He said that an individual named XXXX XXXX was arrested and said that Iwas his accomplice. Skipped to a few days later, XXXX XXXX informed me that there was a court order to freeze my ALL of my funds signed by XXXX XXXX XXXX. I fought for them to not freeze and that's when XXXX XXXX transferred me to XXXX Attorney XXXX XXXX. For the next month, XXXX XXXX would help me with the investigation. At first, he wanted me to fly over to NY to settle this situation in person but I told him that I am middle class forklift driver and do not have the luxury to travel. He then suggested that we do a remote investigation. With the remote investigation, I would have to withdraw money from my XXXX and wire it to them so that they can check with XXXX if there were any criminal funds in my account and that way they can turn my status from suspect to victim. On XX/XX/year>. I wired {$61000.00} to XXXX XXXX XXXX, he received the payment and stated that the investigation will now proceed and that I would get my funds back XX/XX/year>. XXXX XXXX came by and didn't hear anything from him but I just figured that he was busy with XXXX Attorney tasks. About a week later, I decided to contact XXXX XXXX and ask him about the status. He then told me that there were complications during the investigation because they found out that someone opened up another account under my name in Florida. 
So he stated that this investigation would take a little bit longer. Later on, XXXX XXXX stated that XXXX XXXX thought of an idea that we get a loan and wire transfer it to them because they had suspected that XXXX XXXX had accomplices working at major banks and that they might try to frame me by putting criminal funds in my loan, that way the taskforce can immediately marked me as Victim. On XX/XX/year> I wired them {$30000.00}. After that it got quiet, I didn't get any updates from either of them and I got suspicious. Today XX/XX/year> I did some research and found XXXX 's law firm website and gave them a call. A represenative told me that XXXX XXXX has no involvement in this and that I have been scammed.
